title: Leandra humilis
taxonomy: Leandra humilis is classified in the family Melastomataceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the melastome or the memecyclid family. This family is further divided into several subfamilies and genera, with Leandra humilis being a part of it. Leandra humilis is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the tribe Leandreae, which is a part of the subfamily Melastomatoideae. The classification of Leandra humilis is based on its morphological characteristics and molecular phylogenetic analysis. Further research and studies may lead to a more precise classification of this species.
uses: Leandra humilis is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. Research has shown that the extracts of Leandra humilis have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which could be beneficial in treating various health conditions. Additionally, Leandra humilis has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat fever and other infections. Further studies are needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential applications of Leandra humilis.
